title: Ode to Joy
wikitext:
[]: Ode to Joy             Quote Post by Clover » Tue Jan 18, 2011 5:14 pm I don't feel like searching the forums, and google will take too long. What is the version of Ode to Joy in Evangelion? The recording studio or orchestra. I have multiple versions after searching but not that one. I want it.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Jan 18, 2011 5:22 pm isn't it on OST?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Jan 18, 2011 5:33 pm There’s like 500 different soundtrack albums for Evangelion media so I would bet money that it’s somewhere in that catalog.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Thu Jan 20, 2011 3:07 pm You can find it on the "Addition" CD: http://www.discogs.com/Various-Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Addition/release/1900918

[]:             Quote Post by The Lincolnshire Poacher » Thu Jan 20, 2011 4:39 pm Liner notes to my "Addition" CD: Conducted by: ALEXANDER RAHBARI BRTN PHILHARMONIC ORCHESTRA BRUSSELS The liner notes also feature the cover art of the original album the recording came from, too, but if it's just the "Ode to Joy" you want, rather than Beethoven's entire Symphony No. 9, you just have to look as far as getting the Addition CD.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Jan 20, 2011 6:35 pm Thanks, I managed to find it.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jan 21, 2011 8:46 am I'd recommend you hear the whole symphony if you get a chance. I have all 4 movements of the Cleavland Orchestra version of it, which is similar to the orchestration used in Evangelion, and I do believe it is the best piece of music in recorded history.

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Sun May 06, 2012 6:08 pm No...I've studied music, and namely the Choral Ending of the 4th Mvt. of Beethoven's 9th, to a doctorate level, and after way too many versions the most highly respected is Herbert van Karajan with the Berlin Philharmonic. He is the man on late classical and romantic era in German orchestral music.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon May 07, 2012 2:31 am Was the version used in the show recorded specifically for it, or not? I swear I've heard it elsewhere.
